decimal degree.description: a float representation of a degree of rotation

language code.name: language code
language code.description: a string representation of a language

language_code.description: A language code conforming to ISO_639-1

external_identifier.name: external_identifier
external_identifier.description: A CURIE representing an external identifier

string.name: string
notes: In RDF serializations, a slot with range of string is treated as a literal or type xsd:string.   If you are authoring schemas in LinkML YAML, the type is referenced with the lower case "string".

integer.name: integer
notes: If you are authoring schemas in LinkML YAML, the type is referenced with the lower case "integer".

boolean.name: boolean
notes: If you are authoring schemas in LinkML YAML, the type is referenced with the lower case "boolean".

float.name: float
float.description: A real number that conforms to the xsd:float specification
notes: If you are authoring schemas in LinkML YAML, the type is referenced with the lower case "float".

double.name: double
double.description: A real number that conforms to the xsd:double specification
notes: If you are authoring schemas in LinkML YAML, the type is referenced with the lower case "double".

decimal.name: decimal
decimal.description: A real number with arbitrary precision that conforms to the xsd:decimal specification
notes: If you are authoring schemas in LinkML YAML, the type is referenced with the lower case "decimal".

time.name: time
time.description: A time object represents a (local) time of day, independent of any particular day
notes: URI is dateTime because OWL reasoners do not work with straight date or time
If you are authoring schemas in LinkML YAML, the type is referenced with the lower case "time".

date.name: date
date.description: a date (year, month and day) in an idealized calendar
notes: URI is dateTime because OWL reasoners don't work with straight date or time
If you are authoring schemas in LinkML YAML, the type is referenced with the lower case "date".

datetime.name: datetime
datetime.description: The combination of a date and time
notes: If you are authoring schemas in LinkML YAML, the type is referenced with the lower case "datetime".

date_or_datetime.name: date_or_datetime
date_or_datetime.description: Either a date or a datetime
notes: If you are authoring schemas in LinkML YAML, the type is referenced with the lower case "date_or_datetime".

uriorcurie.name: uriorcurie
notes: If you are authoring schemas in LinkML YAML, the type is referenced with the lower case "uriorcurie".

curie.name: curie
notes: If you are authoring schemas in LinkML YAML, the type is referenced with the lower case "curie".
comments: in RDF serializations this MUST be expanded to a URI
in non-RDF serializations MAY be serialized as the compact representation

uri.name: uri
notes: If you are authoring schemas in LinkML YAML, the type is referenced with the lower case "uri".
comments: in RDF serializations a slot with range of uri is treated as a literal or type xsd:anyURI unless it is an identifier or a reference to an identifier, in which case it is translated directly to a node

ncname.name: ncname
notes: If you are authoring schemas in LinkML YAML, the type is referenced with the lower case "ncname".

objectidentifier.name: objectidentifier
objectidentifier.description: A URI or CURIE that represents an object in the model.
notes: If you are authoring schemas in LinkML YAML, the type is referenced with the lower case "objectidentifier".
comments: Used for inheritance and type checking

nodeidentifier.name: nodeidentifier
nodeidentifier.description: A URI, CURIE or BNODE that represents a node in a model.
notes: If you are authoring schemas in LinkML YAML, the type is referenced with the lower case "nodeidentifier".

jsonpointer.name: jsonpointer
jsonpointer.description: A string encoding a JSON Pointer. The value of the string MUST conform to JSON Point syntax and SHOULD dereference to a valid object within the current instance document when encoded in tree form.
notes: If you are authoring schemas in LinkML YAML, the type is referenced with the lower case "jsonpointer".

jsonpath.name: jsonpath
jsonpath.description: A string encoding a JSON Path. The value of the string MUST conform to JSON Point syntax and SHOULD dereference to zero or more valid objects within the current instance document when encoded in tree form.
notes: If you are authoring schemas in LinkML YAML, the type is referenced with the lower case "jsonpath".

sparqlpath.name: sparqlpath
sparqlpath.description: A string encoding a SPARQL Property Path. The value of the string MUST conform to SPARQL syntax and SHOULD dereference to zero or more valid objects within the current instance document when encoded as RDF.
notes: If you are authoring schemas in LinkML YAML, the type is referenced with the lower case "sparqlpath".

A horizon.description: The surface horizon, also called topsoil. It has a defined soil structure, and is mostly made up of humus (decayed organic matter).

B horizon.description: Also known as the subsoil. It is greatly composed of material illuviated (washed in from) layers above it. It is typically denser than the A horizon and has a clayey texture.

C horizon.description: Also known as the substratum is unconsolidated material deepest in the pit and closest to the bedrock.

E horizon.description: Used to refer to subsurface horizons that have undergone a significant loss of minerals, also known as Eluviation (or leaching).

O horizon.description: The organic horizon. Typically at the top of the soil structure and is made up of mostly organic matter.

Permafrost.description: Soil that continuously remains below 0 °C (32 °F) for two years or more.

R layer.description: Hard bedrock, which is usually the lowest layer. It is characterized by tightly bound and unbreakable materials.

read_1_url.name: read_1_url
read_1_url.description: URL for FASTQ file of read 1 of a pair of reads.
read_1_url.title: read 1 FASTQ
comments: If multiple runs were performed, separate each URL with a semi-colon.

External data urls should be available for at least a year. If you would like NMDC to submit your data to an appropriate raw data repository on your behalf please contact us at microbiomedata.science@gmail.com.

cont_well.name: cont_well
cont_well.title: plate position
comments: Required when 'plate' is selected for container type.
Leave blank if the sample will be shipped in a tube.
JGI will not process samples in corner wells, so A1, A12, H1 and H12 will not pass validation.
Fill plate in column-wise format. B1-G1,A2-H2,A3-D3 (NOT A2-A11,B1-B8).

ecosystem.name: ecosystem
ecosystem.description: An ecosystem is a combination of a physical environment (abiotic factors) and all the organisms (biotic factors) that interact with this environment. Ecosystem is in position 1/5 in a GOLD path.
comments: The abiotic factors play a profound role on the type and composition of organisms in a given environment. The GOLD Ecosystem at the top of the five-level classification system is aimed at capturing the broader environment from which an organism or environmental sample is collected. The three broad groups under Ecosystem are Environmental, Host-associated, and Engineered. They represent samples collected from a natural environment or from another organism or from engineered environments like bioreactors respectively.

gold_path_field.name: gold_path_field

tooltip.value: GOLD Ecosystem Classification paths describe the surroundings from which an environmental sample or an organism is collected.

gold_path_field.description: This is a grouping for any of the gold path fields

ecosystem_category.name: ecosystem_category
ecosystem_category.description: Ecosystem categories represent divisions within the ecosystem based on specific characteristics of the environment from where an organism or sample is isolated. Ecosystem category is in position 2/5 in a GOLD path.
comments: The Environmental ecosystem (for example) is divided into Air, Aquatic and Terrestrial. Ecosystem categories for Host-associated samples can be individual hosts or phyla and for engineered samples it may be manipulated environments like bioreactors, solid waste etc.

ecosystem_subtype.name: ecosystem_subtype
ecosystem_subtype.description: Ecosystem subtypes represent further subdivision of Ecosystem types into more distinct subtypes. Ecosystem subtype is in position 4/5 in a GOLD path.
comments: Ecosystem Type Marine (Environmental -> Aquatic -> Marine) is further divided (for example) into Intertidal zone, Coastal, Pelagic, Intertidal zone etc. in the Ecosystem subtype category.

ecosystem_type.name: ecosystem_type
ecosystem_type.description: Ecosystem types represent things having common characteristics within the Ecosystem Category. These common characteristics based grouping is still broad but specific to the characteristics of a given environment. Ecosystem type is in position 3/5 in a GOLD path.
comments: The Aquatic ecosystem category (for example) may have ecosystem types like Marine or Thermal springs etc. Ecosystem category Air may have Indoor air or Outdoor air as different Ecosystem Types. In the case of Host-associated samples, ecosystem type can represent Respiratory system, Digestive system, Roots etc.

env_broad_scale.name: env_broad_scale

tooltip.value: The biome or major environmental system where the sample or specimen originated. Choose values from subclasses of the 'biome' class [ENVO:00000428] in the Environment Ontology (ENVO). For host-associated or plant-associated samples, use terms from the UBERON or Plant Ontology to describe the broad anatomical or morphological context

env_broad_scale.description: In this field, report which major environmental system your sample or specimen came from. The systems identified should have a coarse spatial grain, to provide the general environmental context of where the sampling was done (e.g. were you in the desert or a rainforest?). We recommend using subclasses of ENVO's biome class: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00000428. Format (one term): termLabel [termID], Format (multiple terms): termLabel [termID]|termLabel [termID]|termLabel [termID]. Example: Annotating a water sample from the photic zone in middle of the Atlantic Ocean, consider: oceanic epipelagic zone biome [ENVO:01000033]. Example: Annotating a sample from the Amazon rainforest consider: tropical moist broadleaf forest biome [ENVO:01000228]. If needed, request new terms on the ENVO tracker, identified here: http://www.obofoundry.org/ontology/envo.html
env_broad_scale.title: broad-scale environmental context

mixs_env_triad_field.name: mixs_env_triad_field

tooltip.value: The MIxS environmental triad provides context about where a sample was collected and what it consists of.

mixs_env_triad_field.description: The MIxS environmental triad provides context about where a sample was collected and what it consists of. Its component slots capture the biome that the sample was found in, nearby geographical features that might influence the organisms in the sample, and the substance that was displaced in the process of collecting the sample. Careful population of these slots makes it easier for data users to find samples that are similar, not just in terms of textual values, but through ontological relationships. Furthermore, NMDC requires that its three component slots are populated because the INSDC Biosample databases require that they are populated because the MIxS standard from the GSC requires that they are populated.

id.name: id
id.description: A unique identifier for a thing. Must be either a CURIE shorthand for a URI or a complete URI
notes: abstracted pattern: prefix:typecode-authshoulder-blade(.version)?(_seqsuffix)?
a minimum length of 3 characters is suggested for typecodes, but 1 or 2 characters will be accepted
typecodes must correspond 1:1 to a class in the NMDC schema. this will be checked via per-class id slot usage assertions
minting authority shoulders should probably be enumerated and checked in the pattern

env_local_scale.name: env_local_scale

Expected_value.value: Environmental entities having causal influences upon the entity at time of sampling

tooltip.value: The specific environmental entities or features near the sample or specimen that significantly influence its characteristics or composition. These entities are typically smaller in scale than the broad environmental context. Values for this field should be countable, material nouns and must be chosen from subclasses of BFO:0000040 (material entity) that appear in the Environment Ontology (ENVO). For host-associated or plant-associated samples, use terms from the UBERON or Plant Ontology to describe specific anatomical structures or plant parts.

env_local_scale.description: Report the entity or entities which are in the sample or specimen s local vicinity and which you believe have significant causal influences on your sample or specimen. We recommend using EnvO terms which are of smaller spatial grain than your entry for env_broad_scale. Terms, such as anatomical sites, from other OBO Library ontologies which interoperate with EnvO (e.g. UBERON) are accepted in this field. EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS
env_local_scale.title: local environmental context

env_medium.name: env_medium

tooltip.value: The predominant environmental material or substrate that directly surrounds or hosts the sample or specimen at the time of sampling. Choose values from subclasses of the 'environmental material' class [ENVO:00010483] in the Environment Ontology (ENVO). Values for this field should be measurable or mass material nouns, representing continuous environmental materials. For host-associated or plant-associated samples, use terms from the UBERON or Plant Ontology to indicate a tissue, organ, or plant structure

env_medium.description: Report the environmental material(s) immediately surrounding the sample or specimen at the time of sampling. We recommend using subclasses of 'environmental material' (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00010483). EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS . Terms from other OBO ontologies are permissible as long as they reference mass/volume nouns (e.g. air, water, blood) and not discrete, countable entities (e.g. a tree, a leaf, a table top)
env_medium.title: environmental medium

quad_pos.name: quad_pos

quad_pos.title: quadrant position

radiation_regm.description: Information about treatment involving exposure of plant or a plant part to a particular radiation regimen; should include the radiation type, amount or intensity administered, treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment; can include multiple radiation regimens
radiation_regm.title: radiation regimen

rainfall_regm.name: rainfall_regm

rainfall_regm.title: rainfall regimen

reactor_type.name: reactor_type
reactor_type.description: Anaerobic digesters can be designed and engineered to operate using a number of different process configurations, as batch or continuous, mesophilic, high solid or low solid, and single stage or multistage
reactor_type.title: reactor type

redox_potential.name: redox_potential
redox_potential.description: Redox potential, measured relative to a hydrogen cell, indicating oxidation or reduction potential
redox_potential.title: redox potential
comments: Value must be reported in mV. Provide the numeric portion only.

rel_air_humidity.name: rel_air_humidity
rel_air_humidity.description: Partial vapor and air pressure, density of the vapor and air, or by the actual mass of the vapor and air
rel_air_humidity.title: relative air humidity
comments: Value must be reported in %. Provide the numeric portion only.
percent or float?

rel_humidity_out.name: rel_humidity_out

Preferred_unit.value: gram of air, kilogram of air

rel_humidity_out.description: The recorded outside relative humidity value at the time of sampling
rel_humidity_out.title: outside relative humidity

rel_samp_loc.name: rel_samp_loc
rel_samp_loc.description: The sampling location within the train car
rel_samp_loc.title: relative sampling location

resins_pc.name: resins_pc
resins_pc.description: Saturate, Aromatic, Resin and Asphaltene  (SARA) is an analysis method that divides  crude oil  components according to their polarizability and polarity. There are three main methods to obtain SARA results. The most popular one is known as the Iatroscan TLC-FID and is referred to as IP-143 (source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saturate,_aromatic,_resin_and_asphaltene)
resins_pc.title: resins wt%

rna_isolate_meth.name: rna_isolate_meth
rna_isolate_meth.description: Describe the method/protocol/kit used to extract DNA/RNA.

room_air_exch_rate.name: room_air_exch_rate
room_air_exch_rate.description: The rate at which outside air replaces indoor air in a given space
room_air_exch_rate.title: room air exchange rate

room_count.name: room_count
room_count.description: The total count of rooms in the built structure including all room types

room_dim.name: room_dim
room_dim.description: The length, width and height of sampling room

room_door_share.title: rooms that share a door with sampling room

room_loc.description: The position of the room within the building
room_loc.title: room location in building

room_net_area.name: room_net_area
room_net_area.description: The net floor area of sampling room. Net area excludes wall thicknesses
room_net_area.title: room net area

room_occup.name: room_occup
room_occup.description: Count of room occupancy at time of sampling

comments: Value must be reported in ratio/unitless. Provide the numeric portion only.

room_samp_pos.name: room_samp_pos
room_samp_pos.description: The horizontal sampling position in the room relative to architectural elements
room_samp_pos.title: room sampling position

room_type.name: room_type
room_type.description: The main purpose or activity of the sampling room. A room is any distinguishable space within a structure
room_type.title: room type
room_type.string_serialization: [attic|bathroom|closet|conference room|elevator|examining room|hallway|kitchen|mail room|private office|open office|stairwell|,restroom|lobby|vestibule|mechanical or electrical room|data center|laboratory_wet|laboratory_dry|gymnasium|natatorium|auditorium|lockers|cafe|warehouse]

room_wall_share.name: room_wall_share
room_wall_share.description: List of room(s) (room number, room name) sharing a wall with the sampling room
room_wall_share.title: rooms that share a wall with sampling room

room_window_count.name: room_window_count
room_window_count.description: Number of windows in the room
room_window_count.title: room window count

root_cond.description: Relevant rooting conditions such as field plot size, sowing density, container dimensions, number of plants per container
root_cond.title: rooting conditions

root_med_carbon.name: root_med_carbon
root_med_carbon.description: Source of organic carbon in the culture rooting medium; e.g. sucrose
root_med_carbon.title: rooting medium carbon

root_med_macronutr.name: root_med_macronutr
root_med_macronutr.description: Measurement of the culture rooting medium macronutrients (N,P, K, Ca, Mg, S); e.g. KH2PO4 (170 mg/L)
root_med_macronutr.title: rooting medium macronutrients

root_med_micronutr.name: root_med_micronutr
root_med_micronutr.description: Measurement of the culture rooting medium micronutrients (Fe, Mn, Zn, B, Cu, Mo); e.g. H3BO3 (6.2 mg/L)

root_med_ph.name: root_med_ph
root_med_ph.description: pH measurement of the culture rooting medium; e.g. 5.5

root_med_regl.name: root_med_regl

root_med_solid.name: root_med_solid
root_med_solid.description: Specification of the solidifying agent in the culture rooting medium; e.g. agar
root_med_solid.title: rooting medium solidifier

root_med_suppl.name: root_med_suppl
root_med_suppl.description: Organic supplements of the culture rooting medium, such as vitamins, amino acids, organic acids, antibiotics activated charcoal; e.g. nicotinic acid (0.5  mg/L)
root_med_suppl.title: rooting medium organic supplements

salinity.name: salinity
salinity.description: The total concentration of all dissolved salts in a liquid or solid sample. While salinity can be measured by a complete chemical analysis, this method is difficult and time consuming. More often, it is instead derived from the conductivity measurement. This is known as practical salinity. These derivations compare the specific conductance of the sample to a salinity standard such as seawater
salinity.title: salinity
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: %, mg/L. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

salinity_meth.name: salinity_meth
salinity_meth.description: Reference or method used in determining salinity.
salinity_meth.title: salinity method
comments: Legacy MIxS slot removed from GSC MIxS v6.2.2. Retained for submission-schema compatibility.

salt_regm.name: salt_regm

Preferred_unit.value: gram, microgram, mole per liter, gram per liter

salt_regm.description: Information about treatment involving use of salts as supplement to liquid and soil growth media; should include the name of salt, amount administered, treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment; can include multiple salt regimens
salt_regm.title: salt regimen

samp_collec_device.name: samp_collec_device
samp_collec_device.description: The device used to collect an environmental sample. This field accepts terms listed under environmental sampling device (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO). This field also accepts terms listed under specimen collection device (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GENEPIO_0002094)
samp_collec_device.title: sample collection device
comments: Report dimensions and details when applicable

samp_collec_method.name: samp_collec_method
samp_collec_method.description: The method employed for collecting the sample
samp_collec_method.title: sample collection method
comments: This can be a citation or description

samp_collect_point.name: samp_collect_point
samp_collect_point.description: Sampling point on the asset were sample was collected (e.g. Wellhead, storage tank, separator, etc). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field
samp_collect_point.title: sample collection point

samp_dis_stage.name: samp_dis_stage
samp_dis_stage.description: Stage of the disease at the time of sample collection, e.g. inoculation, penetration, infection, growth and reproduction, dissemination of pathogen
samp_dis_stage.title: sample disease stage

samp_floor.name: samp_floor
samp_floor.description: The floor of the building, where the sampling room is located
samp_floor.title: sampling floor

samp_loc_corr_rate.name: samp_loc_corr_rate
samp_loc_corr_rate.description: Metal corrosion rate is the speed of metal deterioration due to environmental conditions. As environmental conditions change corrosion rates change accordingly. Therefore, long term corrosion rates are generally more informative than short term rates and for that reason they are preferred during reporting. In the case of suspected MIC, corrosion rate measurements at the time of sampling might provide insights into the involvement of certain microbial community members in MIC as well as potential microbial interplays
samp_loc_corr_rate.title: corrosion rate at sample location

samp_mat_process.name: samp_mat_process
samp_mat_process.description: A brief description of any processing applied to the sample during or after retrieving the sample from environment, or a link to the relevant protocol(s) performed
samp_mat_process.title: sample material processing

samp_md.name: samp_md
samp_md.description: In non deviated well, measured depth is equal to the true vertical depth, TVD (TVD=TVDSS plus the reference or datum it refers to). In deviated wells, the MD is the length of trajectory of the borehole measured from the same reference or datum. Common datums used are ground level (GL), drilling rig floor (DF), rotary table (RT), kelly bushing (KB) and mean sea level (MSL). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field
samp_md.title: sample measured depth
comments: Value must be reported in m. Provide the numeric portion only.

samp_name.name: samp_name
samp_name.description: A local identifier or name that for the material sample collected. Refers to the original material collected or to any derived sub-samples.
samp_name.title: sample name
comments: It can have any format, but we suggest that you make it concise, unique and consistent within your lab, and as informative as possible.
If multiple sample environments (soil, water, etc) are used, these values must be unique across all environments.

samp_preserv.name: samp_preserv
samp_preserv.description: Preservative added to the sample (e.g. Rnalater, alcohol, formaldehyde, etc.). Where appropriate include volume added (e.g. Rnalater; 2 ml)
samp_preserv.title: preservative added to sample

samp_room_id.name: samp_room_id
samp_room_id.description: Sampling room number. This ID should be consistent with the designations on the building floor plans
samp_room_id.title: sampling room ID or name

samp_size.name: samp_size
samp_size.description: The total amount (volume or mass) of sample collected.
samp_size.title: amount or size of sample collected
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: L, g, mL, mg. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.
This refers to the TOTAL amount of sample collected from the experiment. NOT the amount sent to each institution or collected for a specific analysis.

samp_sort_meth.name: samp_sort_meth
samp_sort_meth.description: Method by which samples are sorted; open face filter collecting total suspended particles, prefilter to remove particles larger than X micrometers in diameter, where common values of X would be 10 and 2.5 full size sorting in a cascade impactor
samp_sort_meth.title: sample size sorting method

samp_store_dur.name: samp_store_dur
samp_store_dur.description: Duration for which the sample was stored. Indicate the duration for which the sample was stored written in ISO 8601 format
samp_store_dur.title: sample storage duration

samp_store_loc.name: samp_store_loc
samp_store_loc.description: Location at which sample was stored, usually name of a specific freezer/room
samp_store_loc.title: sample storage location

samp_store_temp.name: samp_store_temp
samp_store_temp.description: Temperature at which the sample was stored (degrees are assumed)
samp_store_temp.title: sample storage temperature
comments: Value must be reported in degrees Celsius. Provide the numeric portion only.

samp_subtype.name: samp_subtype
samp_subtype.description: Name of sample sub-type. For example if "sample type" is "Produced Water" then subtype could be "Oil Phase" or "Water Phase". If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field
samp_subtype.title: sample subtype

samp_time_out.name: samp_time_out
samp_time_out.description: The recent and long term history of outside sampling
samp_time_out.title: sampling time outside

samp_transport_cond.name: samp_transport_cond
samp_transport_cond.description: Sample transport duration (in days or hrs) and temperature the sample was exposed to (e.g. 5.5 days; 20   C)
samp_transport_cond.title: sample transport conditions

samp_tvdss.name: samp_tvdss

Expected_value.value: measurement value or measurement value range

samp_tvdss.description: Depth of the sample i.e. The vertical distance between the sea level and the sampled position in the subsurface. Depth can be reported as an interval for subsurface samples e.g. 1325.75-1362.25 m
samp_tvdss.title: sample true vertical depth subsea

samp_type.name: samp_type
samp_type.description: The type of material from which the sample was obtained. For the Hydrocarbon package, samples include types like core, rock trimmings, drill cuttings, piping section, coupon, pigging debris, solid deposit, produced fluid, produced water, injected water, swabs, etc. For the Food Package, samples are usually categorized as food, body products or tissues, or environmental material. This field accepts terms listed under environmental specimen (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GENEPIO_0001246)
samp_type.title: sample type

samp_weather.name: samp_weather
samp_weather.description: The weather on the sampling day
samp_weather.title: sampling day weather

samp_well_name.name: samp_well_name
samp_well_name.description: Name of the well (e.g. BXA1123) where sample was taken
samp_well_name.title: sample well name

sample_link.name: sample_link
sample_link.description: A unique identifier to assign parent-child, subsample, or sibling samples. This is relevant when a sample or other material was used to generate the new sample.
sample_link.title: sample linkage
comments: This field allows multiple entries separated by ; (Examples: Soil collected from the field will link with the soil used in an incubation. The soil a plant was grown in links to the plant sample. An original culture sample was transferred to a new vial and generated a new sample)

sample_shipped.name: sample_shipped
sample_shipped.description: The total amount or size (volume, mass, or area) of sample sent to EMSL.
sample_shipped.title: sample shipped amount
comments: This field is only required when completing metadata for samples being submitted to EMSL for analyses.

sample_type.name: sample_type
sample_type.title: sample type
comments: This can vary from 'environmental package' if the sample is an extraction.

saturates_pc.name: saturates_pc
saturates_pc.description: Saturate, Aromatic, Resin and Asphaltene  (SARA) is an analysis method that divides  crude oil  components according to their polarizability and polarity. There are three main methods to obtain SARA results. The most popular one is known as the Iatroscan TLC-FID and is referred to as IP-143 (source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saturate,_aromatic,_resin_and_asphaltene)
saturates_pc.title: saturates wt%

season.name: season
season.description: The season when sampling occurred. Any of the four periods into which the year is divided by the equinoxes and solstices. This field accepts terms listed under season (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCIT_C94729)
season.title: season

season_environment.name: season_environment
season_environment.description: Treatment involving an exposure to a particular season (e.g. Winter, summer, rabi, rainy etc.), treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment
season_environment.title: seasonal environment

season_precpt.name: season_precpt
season_precpt.description: The average of all seasonal precipitation values known, or an estimated equivalent value derived by such methods as regional indexes or Isohyetal maps
season_precpt.title: average seasonal precipitation
todos: check validation & examples. always mm? so value only? Or value + unit
notes: mean and average are the same thing, but it seems like bad practice to not be consistent. Changed mean to average
comments: Value must be reported in mm. Provide the numeric portion only.
Seasons are defined as spring (March, April, May), summer (June, July, August), autumn (September, October, November) and winter (December, January, February).

season_temp.name: season_temp
season_temp.title: mean seasonal temperature
comments: Value must be reported in degrees Celsius. Provide the numeric portion only.

season_use.name: season_use
season_use.description: The seasons the space is occupied
season_use.title: seasonal use

secondary_treatment.name: secondary_treatment
secondary_treatment.description: The process for substantially degrading the biological content of the sewage
secondary_treatment.title: secondary treatment

sediment_type.name: sediment_type
sediment_type.description: Information about the sediment type based on major constituents
sediment_type.title: sediment type

sewage_type.name: sewage_type
sewage_type.description: Type of wastewater treatment plant as municipial or industrial
sewage_type.title: sewage type

shad_dev_water_mold.name: shad_dev_water_mold
shad_dev_water_mold.description: Signs of the presence of mold or mildew on the shading device
shad_dev_water_mold.title: shading device signs of water/mold

shading_device_cond.name: shading_device_cond
shading_device_cond.description: The physical condition of the shading device at the time of sampling
shading_device_cond.title: shading device condition

shading_device_loc.name: shading_device_loc
shading_device_loc.description: The location of the shading device in relation to the built structure
shading_device_loc.title: shading device location

shading_device_mat.name: shading_device_mat
shading_device_mat.description: The material the shading device is composed of
shading_device_mat.title: shading device material

sieving.name: sieving
sieving.description: Collection design of pooled samples and/or sieve size and amount of sample sieved
sieving.title: sieving
todos: check validation and examples
comments: Describe how samples were composited or sieved.
Use 'sample link' to indicate which samples were combined.

silicate.name: silicate
silicate.title: silicate
comments: Value must be reported in umol/L. Provide the numeric portion only.

size_frac.name: size_frac
size_frac.description: Filtering pore size used in sample preparation
size_frac.title: size fraction selected

size_frac_low.name: size_frac_low
size_frac_low.description: Refers to the mesh/pore size used to pre-filter/pre-sort the sample. Materials smaller than the size threshold are excluded from the sample
size_frac_low.title: size-fraction lower threshold
comments: Value must be reported in um. Provide the numeric portion only.

size_frac_up.name: size_frac_up
size_frac_up.description: Mesh or pore size of the device used to retain the sample. Materials larger than the size threshold are excluded from the sample.
size_frac_up.title: size-fraction upper threshold
comments: Value must be reported in um. Provide the numeric portion only.

slope_aspect.name: slope_aspect
slope_aspect.description: The direction a slope faces. While looking down a slope use a compass to record the direction you are facing (direction or degrees). - This measure provides an indication of sun and wind exposure that will influence soil temperature and evapotranspiration.
slope_aspect.title: slope aspect
comments: Value must be reported in deg. Provide the numeric portion only.
Aspect is the orientation of slope, measured clockwise in degrees from 0 to 360, where 0 is north-facing, 90 is east-facing, 180 is south-facing, and 270 is west-facing.

slope_gradient.name: slope_gradient
slope_gradient.description: Commonly called 'slope'. The angle between ground surface and a horizontal line (in percent). This is the direction that overland water would flow. This measure is usually taken with a hand level meter or clinometer
slope_gradient.title: slope gradient
todos: Slope is a percent. How does the validation work? Check to correct examples
comments: Value must be reported in %. Provide the numeric portion only.

sludge_retent_time.name: sludge_retent_time
sludge_retent_time.description: The time activated sludge remains in reactor
sludge_retent_time.title: sludge retention time
comments: Value must be reported in h. Provide the numeric portion only.

sodium.name: sodium

Preferred_unit.value: milligram per liter, parts per million

sodium.title: sodium
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: [ppm], mg/L. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

soil_horizon.name: soil_horizon
soil_horizon.description: Specific layer in the land area which measures parallel to the soil surface and possesses physical characteristics which differ from the layers above and beneath
soil_horizon.title: soil horizon

soil_text_measure.name: soil_text_measure
soil_text_measure.description: The relative proportion of different grain sizes of mineral particles in a soil, as described using a standard system; express as % sand (50 um to 2 mm), silt (2 um to 50 um), and clay (<2 um) with textural name (e.g., silty clay loam) optional.
soil_text_measure.title: soil texture measurement
comments: Legacy MIxS slot removed from GSC MIxS v6.2.2. Retained for submission-schema compatibility.
Old MIxS used 'quantity value' range but this is clearly structured text, not a single quantity.

soil_texture_meth.name: soil_texture_meth
soil_texture_meth.description: Reference or method used in determining soil texture
soil_texture_meth.title: soil texture method

soil_type.name: soil_type
soil_type.description: Description of the soil type or classification. This field accepts terms under soil (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00001998).  Multiple terms can be separated by pipes
soil_type.title: soil type

soil_type_meth.name: soil_type_meth
soil_type_meth.description: Reference or method used in determining soil series name or other lower-level classification
soil_type_meth.title: soil type method

solar_irradiance.name: solar_irradiance

Preferred_unit.value: kilowatts per square meter per day, ergs per square centimeter per second

solar_irradiance.description: The amount of solar energy that arrives at a specific area of a surface during a specific time interval

comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: kW/m2/d, erg/cm2/s. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

soluble_inorg_mat.name: soluble_inorg_mat

Preferred_unit.value: gram, microgram, mole per liter, gram per liter, parts per million

soluble_inorg_mat.description: Concentration of substances such as ammonia, road-salt, sea-salt, cyanide, hydrogen sulfide, thiocyanates, thiosulfates, etc
soluble_inorg_mat.title: soluble inorganic material

soluble_org_mat.name: soluble_org_mat

Preferred_unit.value: gram, microgram, mole per liter, gram per liter, parts per million

soluble_org_mat.description: Concentration of substances such as urea, fruit sugars, soluble proteins, drugs, pharmaceuticals, etc
soluble_org_mat.title: soluble organic material

soluble_react_phosp.name: soluble_react_phosp

Preferred_unit.value: micromole per liter, milligram per liter, parts per million

soluble_react_phosp.title: soluble reactive phosphorus
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: [ppm], mg/L, umol/L, ug/L. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

source_mat_id.name: source_mat_id

Expected_value.value: for cultures of microorganisms: identifiers for two culture collections; for other material a unique arbitrary identifer

source_mat_id.description: A globally unique identifier assigned to the parent sample or sample that is the source  of this sample.
source_mat_id.title: source material identifier
todos: Currently, the comments say to use UUIDs. However, if we implement assigning NMDC identifiers with the minter we dont need to require a GUID. It can be an optional field to fill out only if they already have a resolvable ID.
comments: Identifiers must be prefixed. Possible FAIR prefixes are IGSNs (http://www.geosamples.org/getigsn), NCBI biosample accession numbers, ARK identifiers (https://arks.org/). These IDs enable linking to derived analytes and subsamples. If you have not assigned FAIR identifiers to your samples, you can generate UUIDs (https://www.uuidgenerator.net/).

Identifiers must be prefixed. Possible FAIR prefixes are: `igsn` for International  Generic Sample Numbers (http://www.geosamples.org/getigsn), `biosample` for NCBI  biosample accession IDs, `gold` for GOLD identifiers.

comments: All depths must be reported in meters. Provide the numerical portion only.

ecosystem.name: ecosystem
ecosystem.description: An ecosystem is a combination of a physical environment (abiotic factors) and all the organisms (biotic factors) that interact with this environment. Ecosystem is in position 1/5 in a GOLD path.
comments: The abiotic factors play a profound role on the type and composition of organisms in a given environment. The GOLD Ecosystem at the top of the five-level classification system is aimed at capturing the broader environment from which an organism or environmental sample is collected. The three broad groups under Ecosystem are Environmental, Host-associated, and Engineered. They represent samples collected from a natural environment or from another organism or from engineered environments like bioreactors respectively.

ecosystem_category.name: ecosystem_category
ecosystem_category.description: Ecosystem categories represent divisions within the ecosystem based on specific characteristics of the environment from where an organism or sample is isolated. Ecosystem category is in position 2/5 in a GOLD path.
comments: The Environmental ecosystem (for example) is divided into Air, Aquatic and Terrestrial. Ecosystem categories for Host-associated samples can be individual hosts or phyla and for engineered samples it may be manipulated environments like bioreactors, solid waste etc.

ecosystem_subtype.name: ecosystem_subtype
ecosystem_subtype.description: Ecosystem subtypes represent further subdivision of Ecosystem types into more distinct subtypes. Ecosystem subtype is in position 4/5 in a GOLD path.
comments: Ecosystem Type Marine (Environmental -> Aquatic -> Marine) is further divided (for example) into Intertidal zone, Coastal, Pelagic, Intertidal zone etc. in the Ecosystem subtype category.

ecosystem_type.name: ecosystem_type
ecosystem_type.description: Ecosystem types represent things having common characteristics within the Ecosystem Category. These common characteristics based grouping is still broad but specific to the characteristics of a given environment. Ecosystem type is in position 3/5 in a GOLD path.
comments: The Aquatic ecosystem category (for example) may have ecosystem types like Marine or Thermal springs etc. Ecosystem category Air may have Indoor air or Outdoor air as different Ecosystem Types. In the case of Host-associated samples, ecosystem type can represent Respiratory system, Digestive system, Roots etc.

elev.name: elev
elev.description: Elevation of the sampling site is its height above a fixed reference point, most commonly the mean sea level. Elevation is mainly used when referring to points on the earth's surface, while altitude is used for points above the surface, such as an aircraft in flight or a spacecraft in orbit
elev.title: elevation, meters
comments: All elevations must be reported in meters. Provide the numerical portion only.
Please use https://www.advancedconverter.com/map-tools/find-altitude-by-coordinates, if needed, to help estimate the elevation based on latitude and longitude coordinates.

env_broad_scale.name: env_broad_scale

tooltip.value: The biome or major environmental system where the sample or specimen originated. Choose values from subclasses of the 'biome' class [ENVO:00000428] in the Environment Ontology (ENVO). For host-associated or plant-associated samples, use terms from the UBERON or Plant Ontology to describe the broad anatomical or morphological context

env_broad_scale.description: In this field, report which major environmental system your sample or specimen came from. The systems identified should have a coarse spatial grain, to provide the general environmental context of where the sampling was done (e.g. were you in the desert or a rainforest?). We recommend using subclasses of ENVO's biome class: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00000428. Format (one term): termLabel [termID], Format (multiple terms): termLabel [termID]|termLabel [termID]|termLabel [termID]. Example: Annotating a water sample from the photic zone in middle of the Atlantic Ocean, consider: oceanic epipelagic zone biome [ENVO:01000033]. Example: Annotating a sample from the Amazon rainforest consider: tropical moist broadleaf forest biome [ENVO:01000228]. If needed, request new terms on the ENVO tracker, identified here: http://www.obofoundry.org/ontology/envo.html
env_broad_scale.title: broad-scale environmental context

env_local_scale.name: env_local_scale

Expected_value.value: Environmental entities having causal influences upon the entity at time of sampling

tooltip.value: The specific environmental entities or features near the sample or specimen that significantly influence its characteristics or composition. These entities are typically smaller in scale than the broad environmental context. Values for this field should be countable, material nouns and must be chosen from subclasses of BFO:0000040 (material entity) that appear in the Environment Ontology (ENVO). For host-associated or plant-associated samples, use terms from the UBERON or Plant Ontology to describe specific anatomical structures or plant parts.

env_local_scale.description: Report the entity or entities which are in the sample or specimen s local vicinity and which you believe have significant causal influences on your sample or specimen. We recommend using EnvO terms which are of smaller spatial grain than your entry for env_broad_scale. Terms, such as anatomical sites, from other OBO Library ontologies which interoperate with EnvO (e.g. UBERON) are accepted in this field. EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS
env_local_scale.title: local environmental context

env_medium.name: env_medium

tooltip.value: The predominant environmental material or substrate that directly surrounds or hosts the sample or specimen at the time of sampling. Choose values from subclasses of the 'environmental material' class [ENVO:00010483] in the Environment Ontology (ENVO). Values for this field should be measurable or mass material nouns, representing continuous environmental materials. For host-associated or plant-associated samples, use terms from the UBERON or Plant Ontology to indicate a tissue, organ, or plant structure

env_medium.description: Report the environmental material(s) immediately surrounding the sample or specimen at the time of sampling. We recommend using subclasses of 'environmental material' (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00010483). EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS . Terms from other OBO ontologies are permissible as long as they reference mass/volume nouns (e.g. air, water, blood) and not discrete, countable entities (e.g. a tree, a leaf, a table top)
env_medium.title: environmental medium

experimental_factor.name: experimental_factor
experimental_factor.description: Variable aspects of an experiment design that can be used to describe an experiment, or set of experiments, in an increasingly detailed manner. This field accepts ontology terms from Experimental Factor Ontology (EFO) and/or Ontology for Biomedical Investigations (OBI)
experimental_factor.title: experimental factor

experimental_factor_other.name: experimental_factor_other
experimental_factor_other.description: Other details about your sample that you feel can't be accurately represented in the available columns.
experimental_factor_other.title: experimental factor- other
comments: This slot accepts open-ended text about your sample.
We recommend using key:value pairs.
Provided pairs will be considered for inclusion as future slots/terms in this data collection template.

geo_loc_name.name: geo_loc_name
geo_loc_name.description: The geographical origin of the sample as defined by the country or sea name followed by specific region name.
geo_loc_name.title: geographic location (country and/or sea,region)

humidity.name: humidity
humidity.description: Amount of water vapour in the air, at the time of sampling
humidity.title: humidity
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: g/m3, %. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

lat_lon.name: lat_lon
lat_lon.description: The geographical origin of the sample as defined by latitude and longitude. The values should be reported in decimal degrees, limited to 8 decimal points, and in WGS84 system
lat_lon.title: geographic location (latitude and longitude)
notes: This is currently a required field but it's not clear if this should be required for human hosts

methane.name: methane

Preferred_unit.value: micromole per liter, parts per billion, parts per million

methane.description: Methane (gas) amount or concentration at the time of sampling
methane.title: methane
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: [ppb], [ppm], umol/L. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

organism_count.name: organism_count
organism_count.description: Total cell count of any organism (or group of organisms) per gram, volume or area of sample, should include name of organism followed by count. The method that was used for the enumeration (e.g. qPCR, atp, mpn, etc.) Should also be provided. (example: total prokaryotes; 3.5e7 cells per ml; qpcr)
organism_count.title: organism count

other_treatment.name: other_treatment
other_treatment.description: Other treatments applied to your samples that are not applicable to the provided fields
other_treatment.title: other treatments
notes: Values entered here will be used to determine potential new slots.
comments: This is an open text field to provide any treatments that cannot be captured in the provided slots.

oxygen.name: oxygen

Preferred_unit.value: milligram per liter, parts per million

oxygen.description: Oxygen (gas) amount or concentration at the time of sampling
oxygen.title: oxygen
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: [ppm], mg/L. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

perturbation.name: perturbation

Expected_value.value: perturbation type name;perturbation interval and duration

perturbation.description: Type of perturbation, e.g. chemical administration, physical disturbance, etc., coupled with perturbation regimen including how many times the perturbation was repeated, how long each perturbation lasted, and the start and end time of the entire perturbation period; can include multiple perturbation types
perturbation.title: perturbation

pollutants.name: pollutants

Preferred_unit.value: gram, mole per liter, milligram per liter, microgram per cubic meter

pollutants.description: Pollutant types and, amount or concentrations measured at the time of sampling; can report multiple pollutants by entering numeric values preceded by name of pollutant
pollutants.title: pollutants

salinity.name: salinity
salinity.description: The total concentration of all dissolved salts in a liquid or solid sample. While salinity can be measured by a complete chemical analysis, this method is difficult and time consuming. More often, it is instead derived from the conductivity measurement. This is known as practical salinity. These derivations compare the specific conductance of the sample to a salinity standard such as seawater
salinity.title: salinity
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: %, mg/L. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

samp_collec_device.name: samp_collec_device
samp_collec_device.description: The device used to collect an environmental sample. This field accepts terms listed under environmental sampling device (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO). This field also accepts terms listed under specimen collection device (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GENEPIO_0002094)
samp_collec_device.title: sample collection device
comments: Report dimensions and details when applicable

samp_collec_method.name: samp_collec_method
samp_collec_method.description: The method employed for collecting the sample
samp_collec_method.title: sample collection method
comments: This can be a citation or description

samp_mat_process.name: samp_mat_process
samp_mat_process.description: A brief description of any processing applied to the sample during or after retrieving the sample from environment, or a link to the relevant protocol(s) performed
samp_mat_process.title: sample material processing

samp_size.name: samp_size
samp_size.description: The total amount (volume or mass) of sample collected.
samp_size.title: amount or size of sample collected
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: L, g, mL, mg. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.
This refers to the TOTAL amount of sample collected from the experiment. NOT the amount sent to each institution or collected for a specific analysis.

samp_store_dur.name: samp_store_dur
samp_store_dur.description: Duration for which the sample was stored. Indicate the duration for which the sample was stored written in ISO 8601 format
samp_store_dur.title: sample storage duration

samp_store_loc.name: samp_store_loc
samp_store_loc.description: Location at which sample was stored, usually name of a specific freezer/room
samp_store_loc.title: sample storage location

samp_store_temp.name: samp_store_temp
samp_store_temp.description: Temperature at which the sample was stored (degrees are assumed)
samp_store_temp.title: sample storage temperature
comments: Value must be reported in degrees Celsius. Provide the numeric portion only.

sample_link.name: sample_link
sample_link.description: A unique identifier to assign parent-child, subsample, or sibling samples. This is relevant when a sample or other material was used to generate the new sample.
sample_link.title: sample linkage
comments: This field allows multiple entries separated by ; (Examples: Soil collected from the field will link with the soil used in an incubation. The soil a plant was grown in links to the plant sample. An original culture sample was transferred to a new vial and generated a new sample)

size_frac.name: size_frac
size_frac.description: Filtering pore size used in sample preparation
size_frac.title: size fraction selected

solar_irradiance.name: solar_irradiance

Preferred_unit.value: kilowatts per square meter per day, ergs per square centimeter per second

solar_irradiance.description: The amount of solar energy that arrives at a specific area of a surface during a specific time interval
solar_irradiance.title: solar irradiance
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: kW/m2/d, erg/cm2/s. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

specific_ecosystem.name: specific_ecosystem
specific_ecosystem.description: Specific ecosystems represent specific features of the environment like aphotic zone in an ocean or gastric mucosa within a host digestive system. Specific ecosystem is in position 5/5 in a GOLD path.
comments: Specific ecosystems help to define samples based on very specific characteristics of an environment under the five-level classification system.

temp.name: temp
temp.description: Temperature of the sample at the time of sampling
temp.title: temperature
comments: Value must be reported in degrees Celsius. Provide the numeric portion only.

ventilation_rate.name: ventilation_rate

Preferred_unit.value: cubic meter per minute, liters per second

ventilation_rate.description: Ventilation rate of the system in the sampled premises
ventilation_rate.title: ventilation rate
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: L/s, m3/min. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

ventilation_type.name: ventilation_type
ventilation_type.description: Ventilation system used in the sampled premises
ventilation_type.title: ventilation type

volatile_org_comp.name: volatile_org_comp

Preferred_unit.value: microgram per cubic meter, parts per million, nanogram per liter

volatile_org_comp.description: Concentration of carbon-based chemicals that easily evaporate at room temperature; can report multiple volatile organic compounds by entering numeric values preceded by name of compound
volatile_org_comp.title: volatile organic compounds

wind_direction.name: wind_direction
wind_direction.description: Wind direction is the direction from which a wind originates
wind_direction.title: wind direction

wind_speed.name: wind_speed
wind_speed.description: speed of wind measured at the time of sampling
wind_speed.title: wind speed
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: km/h, m/s. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

analysis_type.name: analysis_type
analysis_type.description: Select all the data types associated or available for this biosample
analysis_type.title: analysis/data type
comments: MIxS:investigation_type was included as a `see_also` but that term doesn't resolve any more

samp_name.name: samp_name
samp_name.description: A local identifier or name that for the material sample collected. Refers to the original material collected or to any derived sub-samples.
samp_name.title: sample name
comments: It can have any format, but we suggest that you make it concise, unique and consistent within your lab, and as informative as possible.
If multiple sample environments (soil, water, etc) are used, these values must be unique across all environments.

source_mat_id.name: source_mat_id

Expected_value.value: for cultures of microorganisms: identifiers for two culture collections; for other material a unique arbitrary identifer

source_mat_id.description: A globally unique identifier assigned to the parent sample or sample that is the source  of this sample.
source_mat_id.title: source material identifier
todos: Currently, the comments say to use UUIDs. However, if we implement assigning NMDC identifiers with the minter we dont need to require a GUID. It can be an optional field to fill out only if they already have a resolvable ID.
comments: Identifiers must be prefixed. Possible FAIR prefixes are IGSNs (http://www.geosamples.org/getigsn), NCBI biosample accession numbers, ARK identifiers (https://arks.org/). These IDs enable linking to derived analytes and subsamples. If you have not assigned FAIR identifiers to your samples, you can generate UUIDs (https://www.uuidgenerator.net/).

Identifiers must be prefixed. Possible FAIR prefixes are: `igsn` for International  Generic Sample Numbers (http://www.geosamples.org/getigsn), `biosample` for NCBI  biosample accession IDs, `gold` for GOLD identifiers.

alkalinity.name: alkalinity

Preferred_unit.value: milliequivalent per liter, milligram per liter

alkalinity.description: Alkalinity, the ability of a solution to neutralize acids to the equivalence point of carbonate or bicarbonate
alkalinity.title: alkalinity
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: meq/L, mg/L. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

alkyl_diethers.name: alkyl_diethers
alkyl_diethers.title: alkyl diethers
comments: Value must be reported in mol/L. Provide the numeric portion only.

alt.name: alt
alt.description: Heights of objects such as airplanes, space shuttles, rockets, atmospheric balloons and heights of places such as atmospheric layers and clouds. It is used to measure the height of an object which is above the earth's surface. In this context, the altitude measurement is the vertical distance between the earth's surface above sea level and the sampled position in the air
alt.title: altitude
comments: Value must be reported in m. Provide the numeric portion only.

aminopept_act.name: aminopept_act
aminopept_act.title: aminopeptidase activity
comments: Value must be reported in mol/L/h. Provide the numeric portion only.

ammonium.name: ammonium

Preferred_unit.value: micromole per liter, milligram per liter, parts per million

ammonium.description: Concentration of ammonium in the sample
ammonium.title: ammonium
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: [ppm], mg/L, umol/L. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

bacteria_carb_prod.name: bacteria_carb_prod
bacteria_carb_prod.title: bacterial carbon production
comments: Value must be reported in ng/h. Provide the numeric portion only.

biomass.name: biomass
biomass.description: Amount of biomass; should include the name for the part of biomass measured, e.g. Microbial, total. Can include multiple measurements
biomass.title: biomass

bishomohopanol.name: bishomohopanol

Preferred_unit.value: microgram per liter, microgram per gram

bishomohopanol.title: bishomohopanol
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: ug/L, ug/g. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

bromide.name: bromide
bromide.title: bromide
comments: Value must be reported in [ppm]. Provide the numeric portion only.

calcium.name: calcium

Preferred_unit.value: milligram per liter, micromole per liter, parts per million

calcium.description: Concentration of calcium in the sample
calcium.title: calcium
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: [ppm], mg/L, umol/L, mg/kg. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

carb_nitro_ratio.name: carb_nitro_ratio
carb_nitro_ratio.description: Ratio of amount or concentrations of carbon to nitrogen
carb_nitro_ratio.title: carbon/nitrogen ratio
comments: Value must be reported in ratio/unitless. Provide the numeric portion only.

chem_administration.name: chem_administration
chem_administration.description: List of chemical compounds administered to the host or site where sampling occurred, and when (e.g. Antibiotics, n fertilizer, air filter); can include multiple compounds. For chemical entities of biological interest ontology (chebi) (v 163), http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/chebi
chem_administration.title: chemical administration

chloride.name: chloride

Preferred_unit.value: milligram per liter, parts per million

chloride.description: Concentration of chloride in the sample
chloride.title: chloride
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: [ppm], mg/L. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

chlorophyll.name: chlorophyll

Preferred_unit.value: milligram per cubic meter, microgram per liter

chlorophyll.title: chlorophyll
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: mg/m3, ug/L. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

depth.name: depth
depth.description: The vertical distance below local surface. For sediment or soil samples depth is measured from sediment or soil surface, respectively. Depth can be reported as an interval for subsurface samples
depth.title: depth, meters
comments: All depths must be reported in meters. Provide the numerical portion only.

diether_lipids.name: diether_lipids
diether_lipids.description: Concentration of diether lipids; can include multiple types of diether lipids
diether_lipids.title: diether lipids

diss_carb_dioxide.name: diss_carb_dioxide

Preferred_unit.value: micromole per liter, milligram per liter

diss_carb_dioxide.description: Concentration of dissolved carbon dioxide in the sample or liquid portion of the sample
diss_carb_dioxide.title: dissolved carbon dioxide
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: mg/L, umol/L. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

diss_hydrogen.name: diss_hydrogen
diss_hydrogen.title: dissolved hydrogen
comments: Value must be reported in umol/L. Provide the numeric portion only.

diss_inorg_carb.name: diss_inorg_carb

Preferred_unit.value: microgram per liter, milligram per liter, parts per million

diss_inorg_carb.description: Dissolved inorganic carbon concentration in the sample, typically measured after filtering the sample using a 0.45 micrometer filter
diss_inorg_carb.title: dissolved inorganic carbon
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: [ppm], mg/L, ug/L, umol/kg. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

diss_org_carb.name: diss_org_carb

Preferred_unit.value: micromole per liter, milligram per liter

diss_org_carb.description: Concentration of dissolved organic carbon in the sample, liquid portion of the sample, or aqueous phase of the fluid
diss_org_carb.title: dissolved organic carbon
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: mg/L, umol/L, ug/L. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

diss_org_nitro.name: diss_org_nitro
diss_org_nitro.description: Dissolved organic nitrogen concentration measured as; total dissolved nitrogen - NH4 - NO3 - NO2
diss_org_nitro.title: dissolved organic nitrogen
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: mg/L, ug/L. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

diss_oxygen.name: diss_oxygen

Preferred_unit.value: micromole per kilogram, milligram per liter

diss_oxygen.title: dissolved oxygen
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: mg/L, umol/kg, umol/L. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

ecosystem.name: ecosystem
ecosystem.description: An ecosystem is a combination of a physical environment (abiotic factors) and all the organisms (biotic factors) that interact with this environment. Ecosystem is in position 1/5 in a GOLD path.
comments: The abiotic factors play a profound role on the type and composition of organisms in a given environment. The GOLD Ecosystem at the top of the five-level classification system is aimed at capturing the broader environment from which an organism or environmental sample is collected. The three broad groups under Ecosystem are Environmental, Host-associated, and Engineered. They represent samples collected from a natural environment or from another organism or from engineered environments like bioreactors respectively.

ecosystem_category.name: ecosystem_category
ecosystem_category.description: Ecosystem categories represent divisions within the ecosystem based on specific characteristics of the environment from where an organism or sample is isolated. Ecosystem category is in position 2/5 in a GOLD path.
comments: The Environmental ecosystem (for example) is divided into Air, Aquatic and Terrestrial. Ecosystem categories for Host-associated samples can be individual hosts or phyla and for engineered samples it may be manipulated environments like bioreactors, solid waste etc.

ecosystem_subtype.name: ecosystem_subtype
ecosystem_subtype.description: Ecosystem subtypes represent further subdivision of Ecosystem types into more distinct subtypes. Ecosystem subtype is in position 4/5 in a GOLD path.
comments: Ecosystem Type Marine (Environmental -> Aquatic -> Marine) is further divided (for example) into Intertidal zone, Coastal, Pelagic, Intertidal zone etc. in the Ecosystem subtype category.

ecosystem_type.name: ecosystem_type
ecosystem_type.description: Ecosystem types represent things having common characteristics within the Ecosystem Category. These common characteristics based grouping is still broad but specific to the characteristics of a given environment. Ecosystem type is in position 3/5 in a GOLD path.
comments: The Aquatic ecosystem category (for example) may have ecosystem types like Marine or Thermal springs etc. Ecosystem category Air may have Indoor air or Outdoor air as different Ecosystem Types. In the case of Host-associated samples, ecosystem type can represent Respiratory system, Digestive system, Roots etc.

Elevation is mainly used when referring to points on the earth's surface, while altitude is used for points above the surface, such as an aircraft in flight or a spacecraft in orbit
elev.title: elevation, meters
comments: All elevations must be reported in meters. Provide the numerical portion only.
Please use https://www.advancedconverter.com/map-tools/find-altitude-by-coordinates, if needed, to help estimate the elevation based on latitude and longitude coordinates.

magnesium.name: magnesium

Preferred_unit.value: mole per liter, milligram per liter, parts per million, micromole per kilogram

magnesium.description: Concentration of magnesium in the sample
magnesium.title: magnesium
comments: Value must be reported in one of the following units: [ppm], mg/L, mol/L, umol/kg, mg/kg. Provide the numeric portion followed by a space and the unit.

mean_frict_vel.name: mean_frict_vel
mean_frict_vel.title: mean friction velocity
comments: Value must be reported in m/s. Provide the numeric portion only.

mean_peak_frict_vel.name: mean_peak_frict_vel

ecosystem.name: ecosystem
ecosystem.description: An ecosystem is a combination of a physical environment (abiotic factors) and all the organisms (biotic factors) that interact with this environment. Ecosystem is in position 1/5 in a GOLD path.
comments: The abiotic factors play a profound role on the type and composition of organisms in a given environment. The GOLD Ecosystem at the top of the five-level classification system is aimed at capturing the broader environment from which an organism or environmental sample is collected. The three broad groups under Ecosystem are Environmental, Host-associated, and Engineered. They represent samples collected from a natural environment or from another organism or from engineered environments like bioreactors respectively.

ecosystem_category.name: ecosystem_category
ecosystem_category.description: Ecosystem categories represent divisions within the ecosystem based on specific characteristics of the environment from where an organism or sample is isolated. Ecosystem category is in position 2/5 in a GOLD path.
comments: The Environmental ecosystem (for example) is divided into Air, Aquatic and Terrestrial. Ecosystem categories for Host-associated samples can be individual hosts or phyla and for engineered samples it may be manipulated environments like bioreactors, solid waste etc.

ecosystem_subtype.name: ecosystem_subtype
ecosystem_subtype.description: Ecosystem subtypes represent further subdivision of Ecosystem types into more distinct subtypes. Ecosystem subtype is in position 4/5 in a GOLD path.
comments: Ecosystem Type Marine (Environmental -> Aquatic -> Marine) is further divided (for example) into Intertidal zone, Coastal, Pelagic, Intertidal zone etc. in the Ecosystem subtype category.

ecosystem_type.name: ecosystem_type
ecosystem_type.description: Ecosystem types represent things having common characteristics within the Ecosystem Category. These common characteristics based grouping is still broad but specific to the characteristics of a given environment. Ecosystem type is in position 3/5 in a GOLD path.
comments: The Aquatic ecosystem category (for example) may have ecosystem types like Marine or Thermal springs etc. Ecosystem category Air may have Indoor air or Outdoor air as different Ecosystem Types. In the case of Host-associated samples, ecosystem type can represent Respiratory system, Digestive system, Roots etc.

elev.name: elev
elev.description: Elevation of the sampling site is its height above a fixed reference point, most commonly the mean sea level. Elevation is mainly used when referring to points on the earth's surface, while altitude is used for points above the surface, such as an aircraft in flight or a spacecraft in orbit
elev.title: elevation, meters
comments: All elevations must be reported in meters. Provide the numerical portion only.
Please use https://www.advancedconverter.com/map-tools/find-altitude-by-coordinates, if needed, to help estimate the elevation based on latitude and longitude coordinates.

elevator.name: elevator
elevator.description: The number of elevators within the built structure
elevator.title: elevator count

env_broad_scale.name: env_broad_scale

tooltip.value: The biome or major environmental system where the sample or specimen originated. Choose values from subclasses of the 'biome' class [ENVO:00000428] in the Environment Ontology (ENVO). For host-associated or plant-associated samples, use terms from the UBERON or Plant Ontology to describe the broad anatomical or morphological context

env_broad_scale.description: In this field, report which major environmental system your sample or specimen came from. The systems identified should have a coarse spatial grain, to provide the general environmental context of where the sampling was done (e.g. were you in the desert or a rainforest?). We recommend using subclasses of ENVO's biome class: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00000428. Format (one term): termLabel [termID], Format (multiple terms): termLabel [termID]|termLabel [termID]|termLabel [termID]. Example: Annotating a water sample from the photic zone in middle of the Atlantic Ocean, consider: oceanic epipelagic zone biome [ENVO:01000033]. Example: Annotating a sample from the Amazon rainforest consider: tropical moist broadleaf forest biome [ENVO:01000228]. If needed, request new terms on the ENVO tracker, identified here: http://www.obofoundry.org/ontology/envo.html
env_broad_scale.title: broad-scale environmental context

env_local_scale.name: env_local_scale

Expected_value.value: Environmental entities having causal influences upon the entity at time of sampling

tooltip.value: The specific environmental entities or features near the sample or specimen that significantly influence its characteristics or composition. These entities are typically smaller in scale than the broad environmental context. Values for this field should be countable, material nouns and must be chosen from subclasses of BFO:0000040 (material entity) that appear in the Environment Ontology (ENVO). For host-associated or plant-associated samples, use terms from the UBERON or Plant Ontology to describe specific anatomical structures or plant parts.

env_local_scale.description: Report the entity or entities which are in the sample or specimen s local vicinity and which you believe have significant causal influences on your sample or specimen. We recommend using EnvO terms which are of smaller spatial grain than your entry for env_broad_scale. Terms, such as anatomical sites, from other OBO Library ontologies which interoperate with EnvO (e.g. UBERON) are accepted in this field. EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS
env_local_scale.title: local environmental context

env_medium.name: env_medium

tooltip.value: The predominant environmental material or substrate that directly surrounds or hosts the sample or specimen at the time of sampling. Choose values from subclasses of the 'environmental material' class [ENVO:00010483] in the Environment Ontology (ENVO). Values for this field should be measurable or mass material nouns, representing continuous environmental materials. For host-associated or plant-associated samples, use terms from the UBERON or Plant Ontology to indicate a tissue, organ, or plant structure

env_medium.description: Report the environmental material(s) immediately surrounding the sample or specimen at the time of sampling. We recommend using subclasses of 'environmental material' (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00010483). EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS . Terms from other OBO ontologies are permissible as long as they reference mass/volume nouns (e.g. air, water, blood) and not discrete, countable entities (e.g. a tree, a leaf, a table top)
env_medium.title: environmental medium

samp_name.name: samp_name
samp_name.description: A local identifier or name that for the material sample collected. Refers to the original material collected or to any derived sub-samples.
samp_name.title: sample name
comments: It can have any format, but we suggest that you make it concise, unique and consistent within your lab, and as informative as possible.
If multiple sample environments (soil, water, etc) are used, these values must be unique across all environments.

source_mat_id.name: source_mat_id

Expected_value.value: for cultures of microorganisms: identifiers for two culture collections; for other material a unique arbitrary identifer

source_mat_id.description: A globally unique identifier assigned to the parent sample or sample that is the source  of this sample.
source_mat_id.title: source material identifier
todos: Currently, the comments say to use UUIDs. However, if we implement assigning NMDC identifiers with the minter we dont need to require a GUID. It can be an optional field to fill out only if they already have a resolvable ID.
comments: Identifiers must be prefixed. Possible FAIR prefixes are IGSNs (http://www.geosamples.org/getigsn), NCBI biosample accession numbers, ARK identifiers (https://arks.org/). These IDs enable linking to derived analytes and subsamples. If you have not assigned FAIR identifiers to your samples, you can generate UUIDs (https://www.uuidgenerator.net/).

Identifiers must be prefixed. Possible FAIR prefixes are: `igsn` for International  Generic Sample Numbers (http://www.geosamples.org/getigsn), `biosample` for NCBI  biosample accession IDs, `gold` for GOLD identifiers.
